questions.question: What is the REAL difficulty in audio classification, per the lesson?
options: Data: brutal class imbalance, domain shift (clean vs noisy), and label noise — 80% of the problem is curation, augmentation, and evaluation, not architecture
questions.explanation: The 2026 baseline architecture is mature (log-mel → CNN or Transformer → softmax). AudioSet's 100,000:1 long tail across 632 classes is where projects actually live or die.

questions.question: What does the architecture ladder for audio classification look like across the decades?
options: k-NN on flattened MFCCs (1990s, surprisingly strong on small clean sets) → 2D CNN on log-mels treated as images (2015-2019) → AST patchifying the spectrogram into a ViT (2021-2024) → self-supervised BEATs/WavLM (2024-2026)
questions.explanation: The features stayed mels throughout; only the model moved. BEATs-iter3 beats AST by 1-2 mAP on AudioSet at a quarter of the compute, making self-supervised pretraining the 2026 default.

questions.question: What is the "free lunch" baseline for language ID and simple event classification?
options: Training a 70B audio model from scratch
Whisper's encoder frozen with the decoder dropped and a linear classifier attached — near-SOTA with zero audio augmentation
questions.explanation: Whisper's 680k hours of pretraining already encode the discriminative structure; you just read it out. Always try this before training anything.

questions.question: Which techniques actually work against class imbalance?
options: Balanced sampling during training (never in evaluation), mixup (linearly interpolating two clips AND their labels), and SpecAugment (masking random time and frequency bands)
questions.explanation: SpecAugment is described as "simple; critical." Note the asymmetry: rebalance what the model sees in training, but evaluate on the true distribution.

questions.question: How should you pick an evaluation metric for an audio classifier?
options: Match the task structure: top-1/top-5 accuracy for exclusive multiclass (Speech Commands), mAP for multi-label (AudioSet), and per-class recall + macro F1 when heavily imbalanced
WER works for all audio tasks
Use loss value on the training set
questions.explanation: Reference numbers worth knowing: ESC-50 SOTA 97.0% (BEATs-iter3), AudioSet mAP 0.548, Speech Commands v2 at 99.0% (Audio-MAE).
